lit: Bitter to you are the false witnesses whom you set up,
sm: Bitter for you are the false witnesses you set up,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause opening a bitter catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause οὗς ἔστησας modifies ψευδομάρτυρες.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικροί. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: ψευδομάρτυρες. Relative clause verb: ἔστησας.
syn.rhet: The repeated πικρός-anaphora begins a pounding sequence of self-inflicted bitterness.

lit: Bitter to you are the nooses which you prepared,
sm: Bitter for you are the nooses you prepared,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the bitter catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause οὗς ἡτοίμασας modifies βρόχοι.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικροί. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: βρόχοι. Relative clause verb: ἡτοίμασας.
syn.rhet: The refrain continues with instruments of entrapment.

lit: Bitter to you are the scourges which you plaited,
sm: Bitter for you are the scourges you wove,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the continuing catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ἃς ἔπλεξας modifies μάστιγες.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικραί. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: μάστιγες. Relative clause verb: ἔπλεξας.
syn.rhet: The instruments of torment are turned back rhetorically on their makers.

lit: Bitter to you is Judas, whom you paid,
sm: Bitter for you is Judas, whom you hired,
emd: Removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ιούδας.

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the bitter catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ὃν ἐμισθοδότησας modifies Ἰούδας.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικρός. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: Ἰούδας. Relative clause verb: ἐμισθοδότησας.
syn.rhet: The refrain turns from objects to culpable persons, beginning with Judas.

lit: Bitter to you is Herod, whom you followed,
sm: Bitter for you is Herod, whom you followed,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ᾧ ἐξηκολούθησας modifies Ἡρώδης.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικρός. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: Ἡρώδης. Relative clause verb: ἐξηκολούθησας.
syn.rhet: The accusatory litany names hostile rulers as part of Israel's bitterness.

lit: Bitter to you is Caiaphas, to whom you were persuaded,
sm: Bitter for you is Caiaphas, whom you obeyed,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ᾧ ἐπείσθης modifies Καιάφας.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικρός. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: Καιάφας. Relative clause verb: ἐπείσθης.
syn.rhet: Religious authority is added to the chain of bitter collaborators.

lit: Bitter to you is the gall which you prepared,
sm: Bitter for you is the gall you prepared,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ἣν ἐσκεύασας modifies χολή.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικρά. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: χολή. Relative clause verb: ἐσκεύασας.
syn.rhet: The list turns to the bitter elements of the passion itself.

lit: Bitter to you is the vinegar which you produced,
sm: Bitter for you is the vinegar you produced,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ὃ ἐγεώργησας modifies ὄξος.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικρόν. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: ὄξος. Relative clause verb: ἐγεώργησας.
syn.rhet: The bitter drink is folded into the same refrain of culpability.

lit: Bitter to you is the thorn which you made blossom,
sm: Bitter for you is the thorn you brought to bloom,

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause in the catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ἣν ἤνθισας modifies ἄκανθα.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικρά. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: ἄκανθα. Relative clause verb: ἤνθισας.
syn.rhet: The paradoxical flowering of the thorn sharpens the irony.

lit: Bitter to you are the hands which you mangled,
sm: Bitter for you are the hands you mangled,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: πικραΐ -> πικραί; trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause concluding the bitter catalogue.
syn.sub: Relative clause ἃς ἥμαξας modifies χεῖρες.
syn.svo: Implied copula with predicate adjective πικραί. Dative of disadvantage: σοι. Subject: χεῖρες. Relative clause verb: ἥμαξας.
syn.rhet: The refrain culminates in the violated hands of the victim.

src: ἀπέκτεινάς σου τὸν κύριον ἐν μέσῳ ᾿Ιερουσαλήμ.
ana: ἀπέκτεινάς σου τὸν κύριον ἐν μέσῳ Ἰερουσαλήμ.
lit: You killed your Lord in the midst of Jerusalem.
sm: You killed your Lord in the midst of Jerusalem.
emd: Removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ιερουσαλήμ.

syn.mc: Main accusatory clause following the bitter catalogue.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ἀπέκτεινάς. Object: τὸν κύριον with possessive genitive σου. Locative phrase: ἐν μέσῳ Ἰερουσαλήμ.
syn.rhet: The catalogue resolves into a blunt central accusation.

src: Ἀκούσατε πᾶσαι αἱ πατριαὶ τῶν ἐθνῶν" καὶ ἴδετε"
ana: Ἀκούσατε πᾶσαι αἱ πατριαὶ τῶν ἐθνῶν· καὶ ἴδετε·
lit: Hear, all the families of the nations, and see:
sm: Hear, all families of the nations, and look:
emd: Straight OCR quotation marks normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Direct imperative address introducing the public proclamation.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Imperatives: Ἀκούσατε, ἴδετε. Addressed group: πᾶσαι αἱ πατριαὶ τῶν ἐθνῶν.
syn.rhet: The accusation widens from Israel to all nations as witnesses.

ana: καινὸς φόνος γέγονεν ἐν μέσῳ Ἰερουσαλήμ,
lit: A new murder has happened in the midst of Jerusalem,
sm: A new murder has happened in the midst of Jerusalem,

syn.mc: Main declarative clause announcing the crime.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: καινὸς φόνος. Verb: γέγονεν. Locative phrase: ἐν μέσῳ Ἰερουσαλήμ.
syn.rhet: The novelty claim heightens the outrage of the murder.

lit: in a law-governed city,
sm: in a law-governed city,

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase appositional to Jerusalem in line 745.
syn.sub: No finite clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on γέγονεν and elaborates the location.
syn.rhet: The city is qualified by what should have prevented the crime.

lit: in a Hebrew city,
sm: in a Hebrew city,

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase appositional to Jerusalem.
syn.sub: No finite clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on line 745.
syn.rhet: Ethnic-religious identity is added to the city's irony.

lit: in a prophetic city,
sm: in a prophetic city,

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase appositional to Jerusalem.
syn.sub: No finite clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on line 745.
syn.rhet: Prophetic prestige is turned into a source of deeper shame.

tok: pres mp ptcp dat sg f
lit: in a city considered righteous.
sm: in a city thought to be righteous.

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase completing the Jerusalem apposition.
syn.sub: No finite clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on line 745. Predicate adjective with participle: δικαίᾳ νομιζομένῃ.
syn.ptc: νομιζομένῃ is descriptive, qualifying the city's reputed status.
syn.rhet: The climax is reputational irony: the righteous city hosts the unrighteous deed.

src: καὶ τίς πεφόνευται; τίς δὲ ὁ φονεύς;
lit: And who has been murdered? And who is the murderer?
sm: And who has been murdered? And who is the murderer?

syn.mc: Pair of interrogative main clauses.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: First question: subject τίς with verb πεφόνευται. Second question: interrogative predicate τίς with subject ὁ φονεύς.
syn.rhet: The double question dramatizes revelation by delaying the answer.

lit: To say [it] I am ashamed, and to speak [it] I am compelled.
sm: I am ashamed to say it, and yet I am compelled to speak.

syn.mc: Main clause with coordinated infinitival complements.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Verb 1: αἰδοῦμαι with complementary infinitive εἰπεῖν. Verb 2: ἀναγκάζομαι with complementary infinitive λέγειν.
syn.rhet: The speaker stages his reluctance to intensify the horror of what follows.

src: εἰ μὲν γὰρ νύκτωρ γεγόνει ὁ φόνος,
lit: For if the murder had happened by night,
sm: For if the murder had happened at night,

syn.mc: Protasis of a conditional sentence.
syn.sub: Condition completed by line 753; apodosis in line 754.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ φόνος. Verb: γεγόνει. Adverb: νύκτωρ.
syn.rhet: A hypothetical concealment is raised only to be rejected.

tok: perf mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: or had been slaughtered in a wilderness,
sm: or had been slain in the wilderness,

syn.mc: Second protasis member continuing the condition of line 752.
syn.sub: The periphrastic form continues into the apodosis of line 754.
syn.svo: Implied subject: he / the murdered one. Periphrastic verb: ἦν ἐσφαγμένος. Locative phrase: ἐπ᾽ ἐρημίας.
syn.ptc: ἐσφαγμένος forms a periphrastic past passive with ἦν.
syn.rhet: The alternative scenario imagines a hidden or isolated killing.

lit: To be silent was fitting.
sm: Silence would have been fitting.
emd: Removed a stray terminal colon after the period.

syn.mc: Apodosis of the conditional sentence begun in line 752.
syn.sub: Dependent on the εἰ-clause of lines 752-753.
syn.svo: Infinitive subject: σιγᾶν. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: εὔχρηστον.
syn.rhet: The imagined fitness of silence prepares the emphatic νῦν δέ contrast.

src: νῦν δὲ ἐπὶ μέσης πλατείας καὶ ἐν μέσῳ πόλεως
lit: But now on the middle of the square and in the midst of the city
sm: But now in the middle of the square and in the midst of the city

syn.mc: Preposed locative phrase for the main clause in line 757.
syn.sub: No finite clause in this line.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the locative expressions depend on γέγονεν in line 757.
syn.rhet: Public visibility is stressed by stacked central-place expressions.

tok: pres act ptcp gen pl m
lit: at mid-day, with all seeing,
sm: at midday, with everyone watching,

syn.mc: Genitive phrase of time and attendant circumstance dependent on line 757.
syn.sub: No finite clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb; μέσης ἡμέρας marks time, πάντων ὁρώντων marks circumstance.
syn.ptc: ὁρώντων is circumstantial, expressing public witness.
syn.rhet: The murder is made maximally open and undeniable.

lit: An unjust murder of a righteous one has happened.
sm: An unjust murder of a righteous man has happened.

syn.mc: Main clause contrasting with the hypothetical silence of lines 752-754.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ἄδικος φόνος. Verb: γέγονεν. Objective genitive: δικαίου.
syn.rhet: The epigrammatic contrast δικαίου / ἄδικος gives the sentence sharp moral force.

src: καὶ οὕτως ὕψωται ἐπὶ ξύλου ὑψηλοῦ"
ana: καὶ οὕτως ὕψωται ἐπὶ ξύλου ὑψηλοῦ·
lit: And thus he has been lifted up on lofty wood;
sm: And so he has been lifted up on a lofty tree;
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause following the public-murder declaration.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject implied by context. Verb: ὕψωται. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ ξύλου ὑψηλοῦ.
syn.rhet: The public murder is immediately interpreted as elevation on the cross.

src: καὶ τίτλος πρόσκειται τὸν πεφονευμένον σημαίνων.
tok: perf mp ptcp acc sg m
pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And an inscription is affixed, signifying the murdered one.
sm: And an inscription is affixed, indicating the one who has been murdered.

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: τίτλος. Verb: πρόσκειται. Participle: σημαίνων, modifying τίτλος. Object of participle: τὸν πεφονευμένον.
syn.ptc: σημαίνων is explanatory, telling what the inscription does.
syn.rhet: The scene is made judicial and public through the posted title.

src: τίς οὗτος; τὸ εἰπεῖν βαρὺ καὶ τὸ μὴ εἰπεῖν φοβερώτερον.
lit: Who is this one? To say [it is] grievous, and not to say [it is] more fearful.
sm: Who is this? To say it is painful, and not to say it is even more fearful.

syn.mc: Interrogative clause followed by a balanced infinitival comment.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Question: τίς οὗτος. Then two articular infinitive phrases with implied ἐστίν: τὸ εἰπεῖν βαρύ / τὸ μὴ εἰπεῖν φοβερώτερον.
syn.rhet: The paradox of saying and not saying sustains suspense before the next revelation.
